description: Registers latch corrupt data because a hand-coded combinational clock gate glitched the gated clock.
body: Failure mode: a clock gate built from combinational logic produces a brief glitch on the clock when the enable changes, clocking an unintended edge into the gated registers. Detection signals: spurious clock edges in the waveform, data corruption tied to enable transitions, and the gate implemented without an integrated clock-gating cell. Do not gate clocks with raw combinational logic. Safer fallback: replace the gate with a glitch-free integrated clock-gating cell and verify the gated clock has no spurious edges in simulation.
